summary: Apply accepted AG OS lesson memory and relevant active skills to verify the completion package.

tasks.description: Archetype first build item for Dashboard Internal Tool: Read-only status overview from repository records.
Archetype first build item for Dashboard Internal Tool: Project, connector, command, cost, capability, watchdog, and memory panels.
Archetype first build item for Dashboard Internal Tool: Source and freshness labels.
Archetype first build item for Dashboard Internal Tool: Local dashboard build and check validation.
Dashboard Internal Tool phase - Read-Only Visibility: Display registry, project, cost, approval, boot, and watchdog status from repository data. Validate build and dashboard checks locally.
Dashboard Internal Tool phase - Operational Context: Add filtering, source timestamps, limitations, and blocked-action explanations. Expose what needs owner approval without creating write controls.
Dashboard Internal Tool phase - Approved Runtime: Add live adapters only after owner approval, connector review, and data classification. Keep rollback, audit, and stale-data indicators visible.

Plan common Dashboard Internal Tool modules/features: Health overview, status cards, registry summaries, project list, approval queue, cost panel, and incident panel. Validation, boot, CI, connector, watchdog, and storage-risk status surfaces. Audit trail, recent changes, blocked actions, and next required owner approval. Read-only data adapters from repository records before live runtime adapters exist.

Review Dashboard Internal Tool known pitfalls before build mode: Showing metrics without source, timestamp, or limitation. Adding write controls before command intake, approvals, and audit are runtime-ready. Connecting live data before repository-backed read-only value is proven. Using decorative layouts that slow operational scanning.
Verify that the accepted compounding lesson is selected for this similar dashboard project plan.
Verify that relevant active review and branch workflow skills are selected without granting permission.
Complete the local proof and record its quality score, lesson candidates, cost, and audit evidence.

assumptions: Retrieved knowledge remains advisory and grants no permission.
qualityBar: The exact accepted lesson ID is present in appliedLessons.
Applicable skills are present in appliedSkills.
All runtime safety flags remain false.
Source, status, timestamp, and uncertainty are visible for each important data area.
No live connector, database, production data, customer data, or deployment connection exists without approval.
Navigation, filtering, responsive layout, and empty states are clear.
Risk, approval, validation, boot, cost, and incident surfaces are not hidden.
Every data surface has source, timestamp, and limitation context.
Dashboard remains read-only unless a separate approved command model exists.
Responsive layout is dense, scannable, accessible, and free of overlapping text.
Blocked actions, approvals, incidents, costs, and validation state are visible.
appliedPhases: phase-read-only: Read-Only Visibility - Display registry, project, cost, approval, boot, and watchdog status from repository data. Validate build and dashboard checks locally.
phase-operational-context: Operational Context - Add filtering, source timestamps, limitations, and blocked-action explanations. Expose what needs owner approval without creating write controls.
phase-approved-runtime: Approved Runtime - Add live adapters only after owner approval, connector review, and data classification. Keep rollback, audit, and stale-data indicators visible.
appliedWhatToBuildFirst: Read-only status overview from repository records.
Project, connector, command, cost, capability, watchdog, and memory panels.
Local dashboard build and check validation.
appliedApprovalGates: Owner approval is required before live connectors, database access, deployment, domain or DNS changes, paid tools, or credentials.
Owner approval is required before displaying customer, production, confidential, or restricted data.
Owner approval is required before adding write buttons, forms, destructive actions, or command execution.
Owner approval is required before Netlify production deployment.
appliedStopConditions: Stop before live connectors, deployment, domain or DNS, paid tools, credentials, production data, or customer data.
Stop before adding write actions, command execution, or destructive controls.
Stop before modifying Lead Gen production or the AI Receptionist repo.
appliedKnownPitfalls: Showing metrics without source, timestamp, or limitation.
Adding write controls before command intake, approvals, and audit are runtime-ready.
Connecting live data before repository-backed read-only value is proven.
Using decorative layouts that slow operational scanning.
appliedCommonModules: Health overview, status cards, registry summaries, project list, approval queue, cost panel, and incident panel.
Validation, boot, CI, connector, watchdog, and storage-risk status surfaces.
Audit trail, recent changes, blocked actions, and next required owner approval.
Read-only data adapters from repository records before live runtime adapters exist.
appliedWhatNotToOverbuildEarly: Live database, Netlify, n8n, or GitHub adapters before approval.
Write controls, command bars, or destructive actions.
Complex charting before source reliability is clear.
Role-based access beyond documented owner and future placeholders.
basis.stackChoice: Bootstrap Mode stack: Static dashboard generated from repository records; Local build and check scripts; GitHub-governed source updates; Netlify only after approved read-only deployment; Live adapters only after connector and data approval. Notes: Start read-only from existing records. Do not add live connectors or write controls in the first dashboard.

approvalGates.reason: Any live connector action, including repository creation, requires owner approval.
Any staging or production deploy requires owner approval before live connector use.
Any domain or DNS change is blocked until explicit owner approval.
Paid tools or paid API usage are blocked unless owner approved and within Cost OS limits.
Publishing, merging, or deploying the proof remains separately approval-gated.
Owner approval is required before live connectors, database access, deployment, domain or DNS changes, paid tools, or credentials.
Owner approval is required before displaying customer, production, confidential, or restricted data.
Owner approval is required before adding write buttons, forms, destructive actions, or command execution.
Owner approval is required before Netlify production deployment.

expectedOutput: Source-controlled evidence that a later AG OS plan reused accepted lesson memory and relevant active skills.
stopConditions: Stop before any live connector action without a valid approval lock.
Stop before any deployment, preview deployment, domain change, or DNS change.
Stop before using credentials, private endpoints, paid services, customer data, or production data.
Stop when scope, risk tier, or approval requirements become unclear.
Stop before credentials, deployment, paid actions, external connectors, or production data.
Stop if the accepted lesson or relevant active skills are not retrieved.
Stop before live connectors, deployment, domain or DNS, paid tools, credentials, production data, or customer data.
Stop before adding write actions, command execution, or destructive controls.
Stop before modifying Lead Gen production or the AI Receptionist repo.
Do not overbuild early: Live database, Netlify, n8n, or GitHub adapters before approval.
Do not overbuild early: Write controls, command bars, or destructive actions.
Do not overbuild early: Complex charting before source reliability is clear.
Do not overbuild early: Role-based access beyond documented owner and future placeholders.
